tweets.text:
hot take: most "AI SDR" tools are just glorified mail merge with a chatbot slapped on top.

they don't learn. they don't adapt. they don't remember the last conversation.

if your AI forgets a prospect exists after one email, you don't have AI. you have automation with a marketing budget.

there's a massive difference. hot take: most "AI SDR" tools are just glorified mail merge with a chatbot slapped on top.

they don't learn. they don't adapt. they don't remember the last conversation.

honest question for founders:

if your best salesperson quit tomorrow, how long would it take to replace them?

- 3 months?
- 6 months?
- you'd never fully recover the knowledge they took?

tweets.text:
talked to 30+ founders in the last 2 weeks.

every single one had the same problem:

"we generate leads but can't follow up fast enough."

the leads aren't the issue. the speed of response is.

after 5 minutes, your chance of converting drops by 80%.

most teams respond in 24-48 hours.

the math doesn't work. talked to 30+ founders in the last 2 weeks.

tweets.text:
my first company grew to 10 clients without spending a dollar on marketing.

my second company had the best product I ever built. 8M lines of code. 63 days. couldn't get a single sale.

the difference? the first had a referral engine. the second had hope.

hope is not a go-to-market strategy. my first company grew to 10 clients without spending a dollar on marketing.

my second company had the best product I ever built. 8M lines of code. 63 days. couldn't get a single sale.

tweets.text:
Day 37 of building in public.

Most founders compete in existing categories. They fight for 1% market share in a crowded space.

The ones who win? They create the category.

Salesforce created CRM. HubSpot created inbound marketing. Gong created revenue intelligence.

We are creating the AI sales brain - not another tool, not another chatbot.

A system that thinks, learns, adapts, and closes.

If you are building something new, stop trying to fit into someone else's box.

Build the box.

tweets.text:
A dental clinic owner with 42 locations told me his biggest problem isn't getting patients.

It's following up with the ones who already showed interest.

Thousands of warm leads sitting in his system. People who called, asked questions, booked and cancelled.

No one follows up because staff is busy with patients already in the chair.

This is the gap in almost every business. Not lead gen. Lead follow-up.

The fortune is literally in the follow-up and most businesses leave it on the table.

tweets.text:
Stop hiring SDRs.

You spend $60-80K per SDR. Takes 6 months to ramp. They hit quota for maybe 6-8 months. Then they leave.

You just paid $80K for 6 months of mediocre output.

The companies winning right now are not hiring bigger sales teams.

They are building smarter sales systems that learn and compound over time.

The best salesperson is one that never forgets a conversation and never quits.

They are building smarter sales systems that learn and compound over time.

The best salesperson is one that never forgets a conversation and never quits.

#SalesAI #Hiring #Startup
tweets.author: @shubh_dholakiya

tweets.text:
I called 4 Vegas casinos last week.

3 were answered by AI voice agents. Not backup systems - primary phone lines.

Vegas - the most customer-obsessed industry on earth - trusts AI to handle the first touchpoint.

If casinos are doing it, every industry will follow within 2 years.

The companies that figure out AI sales first will eat the ones who wait.

This is not coming. It is here.

tweets.text:
Day 38 of building in public.

learned something this week that changed how I think about content.

the post that got us our biggest lead wasn't a pitch. wasn't a thread. wasn't a hot take.

it was a comment. on someone else's post. about their audience's pain.

one comment. one whale.

the lesson: stop broadcasting. start listening.

the best opportunities don't come from your timeline. they come from other people's replies. Day 38 of building in public.

tweets.text:
the founder who builds and the founder who sells are rarely the same person.

but the founder who builds a system that sells?

that's a different animal entirely.

you don't need a co-founder. you need a system that does what a co-founder would.

the leverage is insane when you figure this out. the founder who builds and the founder who sells are rarely the same person.

but the founder who builds a system that sells?

that's a different animal entirely.

you don't need a co-founder. you need a system that does what a co-founder would.

the leverage is insane when you figure this out.

tweets.text:
the best cold emails I've seen don't sell anything.

they ask one question that proves you understand the prospect's world better than their own team does.

that's it. one question. no pitch. no CTA. no "let me know if you'd like to chat."

just proof that you did your homework.

the meeting books itself after that. the best cold emails I've seen don't sell anything.

tweets.text:
AI agents can think.
But they can't reliably share their skills.

Agents are stuck reinventing the same capabilities.
No universal system for discovering skills.
No persistent infrastructure for machine capability.

That's a massive bottleneck for the agentic internet. ↓ AI agents can think.

tweets.text:
El CEO de ClickUp acaba de dejarlo claro: el futuro del software no es añadir funciones infinitas, es la CONVERGENCIA a través de la IA. El CEO de ClickUp acaba de dejarlo claro: el futuro del software no es añadir funciones infinitas, es la CONVERGENCIA a través de la IA.


Zeb Evans (@DJ_CURFEW)

"I will learn to code and I will create a BILLION dollar idea with this laptop."
I wrote that in 2013.
I bet EVERYTHING on that laptop.

I built a business that generated almost $3m cash. Then I shut it down overnight.

And I bet it ALL on ClickUp. Every cent.
I bet on our team.
I bet on moving to Silicon Valley.
A house we could barely afford.
5am standups. Building something nobody else could see.

Every bet got BIGGER.

Today, some say it paid off.
1,300+ employees. 3,200+ agents. Multi-billion-dollar valuation.

But I'm not done.

My message to our company: GO ALL IN.
I'm betting the entire company on what I see as the future of software and AI - CONVERGENCE.

But that's how it's always felt. Right before the bet hits.

If you're sitting there with your own laptop, your own crazy idea, and everyone thinks you're nuts...

Bet on it.

What was your bet?




— https://nitter.net/DJ_CURFEW/status/2024532853346730352#m

tweets.text:
La mayoría de las IAs hacen cosas por ti.

Los AI Selves de Pika son tú.

Construidos a partir de tu personalidad, tu voz, tu apariencia. Con memoria persistente y presencia autónoma en X, Slack, Discord.

Únete a la lista en http://pika.me 🫡 La mayoría de las IAs hacen cosas por ti.

Los AI Selves de Pika son tú.

Construidos a partir de tu personalidad, tu voz, tu apariencia. Con memoria persistente y presencia autónoma en X, Slack, Discord.

Únete a la lista en pika.me 🫡



Pika (@pika_labs)

Introducing Pika AI Selves: AI you birth, raise, and set loose to be a living extension of you. They’re rich, multi-faceted beings with persistent memory, and maybe even a peanut allergy. It’s up to you!  

Have them send pictures to your group chat. Make a video game about your fish. Call your mom while you do anything but call your mom. The possibilities are as myriad as the stars ✨ 

Get on the list to give birth to yours at pika dot me

Video
  




— https://nitter.net/pika_labs/status/2024919175878377587#m

tweets.text:
Hot take: La "inspiración" siempre ha sido un remix humano. Hot take: La "inspiración" siempre ha sido un remix humano.


Buzzy Now (@Buzzy_now_AI)

Seedance 2.0 clone is the wrong way！
Big studios like Disney aren’t fighting AI.
They’re fighting simple copycats.

What creators really want is their own fresh stories referenced on the viral structure of blockbusters, not simply clones.
Buzzy is built for this.

It learns the essence of viral videos then recreates them 10x more engaging.
Not just clone, outshine it with better creativity!

RT + Comment "Buzzy" to get free access for 30 days.

Video
  




— https://nitter.net/Buzzy_now_AI/status/2024866375735631909#m

tweets.text:
R to @arceyul: 3. Con el video viral ganador que elegiste, Buzzy no se detiene en el análisis.

🎯 Te da el PLAN DE CONTENIDO exacto:

- Gancho ganador.
- Narrativa paso a paso.
- Copy y escenas.
- Dirección visual (el mismo patrón viral).

Remezcla tu producto con el mismo formato probado. Sin adivinanzas. 3. Con el video viral ganador que elegiste, Buzzy no se detiene en el análisis.

tweets.text:
Todos están OBSESIONADOS con OpenClaw.

Terminal.
Claves API.
Depuración infinita.
Tokens quemándose con cada error.

Mientras ellos configuran… yo estoy cerrando clientes.

Uso los conectores MCP de Anthropic en Claude para ejecutar propuestas completas con @GammaApp en minutos.

Sin código.
Sin fricción.
Sin drama técnico.

Mi stack:

• Gmail → notas de discovery
• Notion → brief del cliente
• Gamma → presentación final

Le pedí a Claude que:

1. Escaneara Gmail y extrajera los requisitos clave.
2. Tomara el resumen del cliente en Notion.
3. Construyera una propuesta completa en Gamma con mi estilo guardado.

Resultado:

Presupuesto desglosado.
Cronograma claro.
Entregables alineados.
Narrativa lista para enviar.

Tiempo total: menos de 2 minutos.

¿Cambios? Edito en Gamma.
¿Nuevo formato? Remix a carrusel.
¿Otro idioma? Traducción completa con una sola instrucción.

Sin copiar y pegar.
Sin formateo manual.
Sin loops de debugging.

Esto es Claude con Conectores trabajando de verdad.

Mientras otros siguen ajustando su entorno…
yo ya envié la propuesta.

Conecta tus apps.
Dale contexto a Claude.
Y deja que el trabajo avance solo.

Pruébalo ahora: https://gamma.app/?utm_medium=creator&utm_content=Marco_IA Todos están OBSESIONADOS con OpenClaw.

Sonnet 4.6 is better than this model and a fifth of the price.

tweets.text:
R to @AlexFinn: Notes on my planned setup:

Kimi K2.5 running across both Mac Studios using @exolabs Also will have MiniMax 2.5 running at the same time. Opus 4.6 will power Henry, the main orchestrator. The local MiniMax will power the agent on the Mac Mini. Kimi K2.5 will power the 2nd Studio Notes on my planned setup:

Kimi K2.5 running across both Mac Studios using @exolabs Also will have MiniMax 2.5 running at the same time. Opus 4.6 will power Henry, the main orchestrator. The local MiniMax will power the agent on the Mac Mini. Kimi K2.5 will power the 2nd Studio
